Q10 MCQ Not checked
General Aptitude • ELECTRICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

In film and videotape, it is possible to induce viewers to project their feelings onto characters on the screen. In one study, when a camera shot of a woman’s face was preceded by a shot of a baby in a crib, the audience thought the woman’s face was registering happiness. When the same shot of the woman’s face was preceded by a shot of a lion running toward the camera, the audience thought the woman’s face was registering fear. Television news teams must be careful to avoid such manipulation of their viewers.
Which of the following can be inferred from the passage?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
This is an inference question. The test writers are probably not interested in the conclusion of the passage. Considering the given options, we’ll look for a statement that seems so obvious that it almost doesn’t need saying.
• Television news teams have abused their position of trust in the past. If you chose this answer,
you inferred too much. The passage doesn’t say that news teams have ever abused their position
of trust. Eliminate it.
• The expression on the woman’s face was, in actuality, blank. The audience had no idea what
the expression on the woman’s face was, and neither do we.
• A camera shot of a baby in a crib provoked feelings of happiness in the audience. This is the
best answer. The passage says that the audience projects its own feelings onto characters on
the screen. If the audience believes the woman’s face reflects happiness, then that must have
been its own reaction.
• Audiences should strive to be less gullible. Rather it suggests Television news teams to be
careful to avoid manipulation of their viewers. This statement goes way beyond the intent of the passage.
Hence, option (c) is correct.

Q28 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
S1 : In Deterministic Quick Sort, an adversary can always provide an input that leads to Ο(n2) time.
S2 : In Selection Sort, the position of the minimum element found in each pass does not depend on previous swaps, which makes its running time independent of the initial ordering of elements.
Which of the above statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
• Deterministic Quick Sort chooses pivots using a fixed rule (for example, always the first element).
An adversary can therefore feed a sorted or reverse-sorted array, ensuring each partition is
maximally unbalanced. Time complexity becomes Ο(n2). So, S1 is true.
• In Selection Sort, each pass scans the entire unsorted portion to locate the minimum. The
algorithm’s number of comparisons does not depend on whether the array is already sorted,
partially sorted, or completely random. The “position” of the next minimum is found purely
by comparison, unaffected by previous swaps hence the running time is independent of initial
ordering. So, S2 is true.

Q43 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following graphical representation of relation:

 
Which of the above graph represent partial ordered relation?
Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
In (i) graph:
• Self loop present on every vertex. So reflexive.
• Since all arrows except self loop are one way. So antisymmetric.
• Since (a, b) and (b, d) present but (a, d) edge not present. So, not transitive relation. Hence not
partial order relation.
In (ii) graph:
• Self loop present for every vertex. So reflexive.
• Since all arrows except self loop are one way, so it is antisymmetric.
• Since (c, a) and (a, d) edge present but (c, d) edge not present. So, not transitive relation.
Hence not partial order relation.

Q47 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following statements is/are true?

Correct answer: A, D
Solution

(a, d)
• Input output processor mode uses separate bus for memory and I/O unit.
• Memory mapped I/O mode uses common bus with common control signal for both I/O and
memory unit.
• Isolated I/O mode uses common bus but different control signal for both I/O and memory
unit.

Q54 NAT Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Consider a database system that uses a basic timestamp-based concurrency control protocol. Let T1, T2, …, Tn be concurrent transactions with distinct timestamps.
Consider the following statements:
I. The protocol cannot enter deadlock, even if multiple transactions simultaneously request conflicting operations.
II. A transaction with an older timestamp may never complete if it repeatedly conflicts with younger transactions.
III. In the protocol, a read operation is rejected if a younger transaction has already written the
same data item.
IV. The protocol guarantees strict first-come-first-serve order of transaction execution.
How many number of statements are correct?

NAT
Correct answer: 3
Solution

3
• Deadlocks occur when transactions wait in a cycle for resources (as in 2PL). In timestamp
protocol, transactions that violate timestamp ordering are aborted immediately, not made to
wait. Hence deadlock is impossible.
• Older transactions may keep getting aborted if younger transactions consistently conflict
with their operations. This is called starvation.
• If a younger transaction wrote a data item, an older transaction trying to read it violates
timestamp order. The older transaction is aborted or restarted, so the read is rejected.
• Execution depends on timestamps, not actual arrival order. A transaction that arrives first
may have a later timestamp than another, so first-come-first-serve is not guaranteed.

Q56 NAT Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +2 / -0
Not attempted

How many of the following statements about finite languages over some finite alphabet Σ are true.
A. It could be undecidable.
B. It is Turing Machine recognizable.
C. It is constant sensitive language.
D. Every finite subset of a regular language is regular.
E. Every finite subset of a non-regular language is regular.

NAT
Correct answer: 4
Solution

4
Every finite set is regular and hence also a CSL and Turing recognizable.
Hence, B, C, D, E are correct.

Q57 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Which of the following situations is most likely to cause instability in a distributed routing algorithm even when all routers periodically exchange updates?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
When routers compute routes based on incomplete or inconsistent topology information. In
asynchronous distributed routing, routers update at different times, causing temporary mismatches
in their routing tables. This leads to route oscillation, loops, and slow convergence, making the
algorithm unstable.

Q58 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Which of the following statements regarding LR(0) parser is FALSE?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
No grammar with empty production can be LR(0). But empty rules are allowed in regular grammar
as the only condition for a grammar to be regular is to be either left linear or right linear.
